name: route
description: Manually route a query to the optimal Claude model (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus)
capabilities: Route queries to Haiku for simple, quick tasks
Route queries to Sonnet for typical coding tasks
Route queries to Opus for complex analysis
Validate model names and show help for invalid inputs
Support both short names and full model names
Prefix responses with model override information
author: 0xrdan
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill with no code execution, file access, network calls, or external commands. The skill defines argument parsing logic and subagent routing behavior for the Claude Router system. All static findings are false positives: metadata URLs flagged as network access, markdown code fences flagged as backtick execution, and routing terminology flagged as C2/crypto patterns.
valueStatement: Claude automatically selects models based on query complexity. This skill lets you override that selection and force a specific model for precise control over capability and cost.
limitations: Does not change the underlying model capabilities
Cost savings depend on choosing the appropriate model
Cannot route to models outside the Claude family

useCases.title: Reduce API costs
useCases.description: Force Haiku for simple queries to save on API costs while maintaining quality

useCases.title: Ensure deep analysis
useCases.description: Force Opus when you need maximum reasoning capability for difficult problems

useCases.title: Standardize workflows
useCases.description: Enforce Sonnet as your team's default model for consistent results

promptTemplates.title: Quick question
promptTemplates.prompt: /route haiku What is the capital of France?

promptTemplates.title: Code implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: /route sonnet Write a function to validate email addresses in Python

promptTemplates.title: Complex analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: /route opus Analyze the security implications of this authentication flow

promptTemplates.title: Explicit Sonnet
promptTemplates.prompt: /route standard Design a caching system for a web application

bestPractices: Match model complexity to task complexity for optimal cost-performance balance
Use Opus for multi-step reasoning tasks that require deep analysis
Test with Haiku first for simple queries to verify expected quality
antiPatterns: Using Opus for trivial questions wastes resources
Forcing Haiku for complex problems leads to poor results
Ignoring the model's strengths for specific task types

faq.question: Which models are supported?
faq.answer: Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus. Short names (haiku, sonnet, opus) and aliases (fast, standard, deep) both work.

faq.question: What happens with an invalid model?
faq.answer: The skill displays usage help with available models and examples for correct syntax.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude Code CLI tool for terminal-based model routing.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is prompt-only with no code execution, file access, or network calls.

faq.question: Why use manual routing?
faq.answer: Control costs, ensure specific capability levels, or standardize team workflows across different task types.

faq.question: How does this differ from auto-selection?
faq.answer: Auto-selection estimates complexity automatically. Manual routing gives you explicit control when you know the best model for your needs.
